author: śrīla rūpa gosvāmī
verses:
śrī⋅kṛṣṇaḥ paramānando govindo nanda⋅nandanaḥ
tamāla⋅śyāmala⋅ruciḥ śikhaṇḍa⋅kṛta⋅śekharaḥ

pīta⋅kauśeya⋅vasano madhura⋅smita⋅śobhitaḥ
kandarpa⋅koṭi⋅lāvaṇyo vṛndāraṇya⋅mahotsavaḥ

vaijayantī⋅sphurad⋅vakṣāḥ kakṣātta⋅laguḍottamaḥ
kuñjārpitaratir guñjā⋅puñja⋅mañjula⋅kaṇṭhakaḥ

karṇikārāḍhya⋅karṇa⋅śrī⋅r⋅dhṛta⋅svarṇābha⋅varṇakaḥ
muralī⋅vādana⋅paṭur vallavī⋅kula⋅vallabhaḥ

gāndharvāpti⋅mahā⋅parvā rādhārādhana⋅peśalaḥ
iti śrī⋅kṛṣṇa⋅candrasya nāma viṁśati⋅saṁjñakam

ānandākhyaṁ mahā⋅stotraṁ jaḥ paṭhec chṛṇuyāc ca jaḥ
sa paraṁ saukhyam āsādya kṛṣṇa⋅prema⋅samanvitaḥ

sarva⋅loka⋅priyo bhūtvā sad⋅guṇāvali⋅bhūṣitaḥ
vraja⋅rāja⋅kumārasya sannikarṣam avāpnuyāt
en_translation:
Śrī Kṛṣṇa, the Supreme Bliss, Govinda, the Son of Nanda. His luster is dark like the Tamāla; He has made a crown of a peacock feather.

He wears yellow silk cloth; He is adorned with a sweet smile. His beauty is that of millions of Cupids; He is the great festival of the forest of Vṛndā.

His chest shines with the Vaijayantī garland; He holds an excellent staff under His arm. He is attached to the groves; His throat is charming with a cluster of Guñjā.

His ears are wealthy with the Karṇikāra; He wears golden-colored pigments. He is expert in playing the flute; He is the beloved of the community of cowherd women.

He is the great festival of the attainment of Gāndharvā; He is expert in the worship of Rādhā. Thus, these are the twenty names of Śrī Kṛṣṇa⋅candra.

He who reads and he who hears this great Stotra named 'Ānanda' (Bliss), attaining supreme happiness, endowed with love for Kṛṣṇa.

Becoming dear to all people, adorned with a multitude of good qualities, he attains the vicinity of the Prince of Vraja.
